key_themes: Skyrizi and Rinvoq driving robust 2026 revenue and 45% EPS growth
Successful post-Humira transition with $67B revenue guidance
Attractive valuation, high dividend yield (~3.3%), and strong cash flow
Analyst Buy consensus with ~$249 average price target (~20% upside)
Near-term competitive pressures in immunology and one-time Q1 expenses
summary: AbbVie is viewed positively by investors and analysts for its strong 2026 outlook, with Skyrizi and Rinvoq now generating more revenue than peak Humira and powering projected EPS of ~$14.50. The stock has faced some YTD pressure and trades near $208–210 with a high earnings bar ahead of Q1 results on April 29, but the combination of a 3.3% dividend yield, robust free cash flow, and Buy-rated analyst targets around $249 supports a 'buy the dip' narrative. Sentiment is solidly positive on the long-term fundamentals and successful immunology franchise pivot, despite competitive threats from oral therapies and one-time IPR&D charges.[[1]](https://www.tikr.com/blog/abbvie-stock-eps-set-to-jump-45-in-2026-as-skyrizi-and-rinvoq-dominate-ibd)[[2]](https://seekingalpha.com/article/4888680-abbvie-a-high-2026-earnings-bar-but-shares-are-attractive-on-valuation)[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ABBV/)
sources: 15+ web news/articles (Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, Reuters, Zacks, Motley Fool, TIKR), ~25 recent X posts analyzed (mostly Latest mode since 2026-01-01), Multiple Reddit threads (r/dividends, r/StockInvest, r/stockstobuytoday)
